title: optimize_distinct_in_order may drastically slowdown some queries
description:
### Company or project name
https://www.innervate.com/
### Describe the unexpected behaviour
Query executes 27 seconds with `optimize_distinct_in_order` and 84 milliseconds without.
25.8.8.26
```sql
create table T(A UInt64, B UInt64) Engine = MergeTree() order by A as select *, 1 from numbers(5e5);
create table item_dict_t(id UInt64, a1 UInt64, a2 Int32) Engine=MergeTree order by id as select 1,1,1;
CREATE DICTIONARY item_dict ( id UInt64, a1 UInt64, a2 Int32)
PRIMARY KEY id SOURCE(CLICKHOUSE(TABLE item_dict_t DB 'default'))
LIFETIME(MIN 0 MAX 0) LAYOUT(HASHED());
SELECT DISTINCT
A,
dictGetUInt64('item_dict', 'a1', toUInt64(B)) AS a1,
dictGetInt32('item_dict', 'a2', toUInt64(B)) AS a2
FROM
(
SELECT A, B FROM T
) FORMAT `Null`
0 rows in set. Elapsed: 27.406 sec. Processed 500.00 thousand rows, 8.00 MB (18.24 thousand rows/s., 291.91 KB/s.)
Peak memory usage: 46.96 MiB.
set optimize_distinct_in_order=0;
SELECT DISTINCT
A,
dictGetUInt64('item_dict', 'a1', toUInt64(B)) AS a1,
dictGetInt32('item_dict', 'a2', toUInt64(B)) AS a2
FROM
(
SELECT A, B FROM T
) FORMAT `Null`
0 rows in set. Elapsed: 0.084 sec. Processed 500.00 thousand rows, 8.00 MB (5.96 million rows/s., 95.29 MB/s.)
Peak memory usage: 117.96 MiB.
drop dictionary item_dict;
drop table item_dict_t;
drop table T;
```
### Which ClickHouse versions are affected?
25.3+
### How to reproduce
above
### Expected behavior
_No response_
### Error message and/or stacktrace
_No response_
### Additional context
```
SELECT DISTINCT
A,
dictGetUInt64('item_dict', 'a1', toUInt64(B)) AS a1,
dictGetInt32('item_dict', 'a2', toUInt64(B)) AS a2
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE T1(A BIGINT, B DOUBLE) AS SELECT number, number * 1.5 FROM generate_series(1, 100000);
CREATE TABLE T2(C VARCHAR, D INT) AS SELECT CAST(number AS VARCHAR) AS C, CASE WHEN number % 2 = 0 THEN number ELSE NULL END AS D FROM generate_series(1, 1000);
CREATE TABLE item_dict2 AS SELECT id, a1, a2 FROM (SELECT id, a1, a2 FROM T2) AS subquery;
SELECT DISTINCT A, (SELECT a1 FROM item_dict2 WHERE id = CAST(B AS BIGINT)) AS a1, (SELECT a2 FROM item_dict2 WHERE id = CAST(B AS BIGINT)) AS a2 FROM (SELECT A, B FROM T1);
